name: Negratín Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse del Negratín por la Hoya de Baza — circular desde Pozo Alcón
description: This difficult 62.8-mile road cycling route circles Embalse del Negratín through the Hoya de Baza, offering diverse desert and lake views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Pozo Alcón offers diverse terrain within Andalusia, serving as a southern gateway to the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The region features quiet mountain roads, expansive pine forests, and dramatic valleys, transitioning to arid areas with olive groves. Cyclists encounter a mix of paved and unpaved sections, with routes winding through high plateaus and past significant reservoirs. This varied landscape provides a range of elevation profiles suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Pozo Alcón The most popular road cycling route is Granada Geopark: Pozo Alcón and Castril Loop, a 54.9 miles (88.3 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route explores the varied landscapes of the Granada Geopark. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Negratín Reservoir Loop, a difficult 62.8 miles (101.0 km) path. This route offers views of the large Negratín Reservoir and surrounding terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Portillo Reservoir and Hoya de Baza Loop, a 43.6 miles (70.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the Portillo Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Pozo Alcón is defined by quiet mountain roads, extensive pine forests, and vast olive groves. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Pozo Alcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pozo Alcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pozo Alcón?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;13&lt;/strong&gt; road cycling routes around Pozo Alcón on komoot, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 6 moderate, and 6 difficult options, ensuring there's a ride for every preference.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Pozo Alcón?
A: Pozo Alcón offers a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll encounter quiet mountain roads, expansive pine forests, and dramatic valleys, especially as you approach the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The terrain transitions to more arid areas with vast olive groves and high plateaus further south. While primarily paved, some routes may feature well-maintained unpaved sections, providing a varied and engaging ride.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Pozo Alcón?
A: Yes, for those looking for an easier ride, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1344743455/fontanar-y-cuevas-del-campo-circular-desde-pozo-alcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanar y Cuevas del Campo — circular desde Pozo Alcón&lt;/a&gt; is classified as easy. This 29.5 km circular route offers a more relaxed experience, perfect for families or those new to road cycling in the region.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling in Pozo Alcón?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4473525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt;, or even catch glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Guadalquivir River&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer panoramic views from points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/183641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilillo&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in Sierra de Cazorla, and pass by impressive reservoirs like the Guadalentín Reservoir.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Pozo Alcón?
A: The best time to visit Pozo Alcón for road cycling is during the mild spring (April to June) and fall (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler temperatures of winter, making for ideal cycling conditions.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350371720/granada-geopark-pozo-alcon-and-castril-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark: Pozo Alcón and Castril Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343621484/negratin-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options for experienced cyclists.

Q: What is the difficulty level of road cycling routes in Pozo Alcón?
A: The routes around Pozo Alcón cater to a range of abilities. You'll find 1 easy route, 6 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes. The difficult routes often feature significant elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Tíscar – Quesada loop from Pozo Alcón&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1,400 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Pozo Alcón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet mountain roads, the stunning natural scenery of the Sierras de Cazorla, and the diverse terrain that offers both challenging climbs and picturesque touring routes.

Q: Can I expect to see any wildlife while cycling in the region?
A: Yes, as Pozo Alcón is a southern gateway to the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, you have a good chance of encountering diverse wildlife. The park is known for its rich fauna, including various bird species and other animals, especially in the more secluded forested areas.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer breathtaking views, particularly those that climb into the higher elevations of the Sierra de Cazorla. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343627302/portillo-reservoir-and-hoya-de-baza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Reservoir and Hoya de Baza Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides scenic vistas of the reservoir and surrounding landscapes. Keep an eye out for panoramic viewpoints that showcase the vast olive groves and mountainous terrain.
tips: Bluer than blue. The water in this landscape - breathtaking

Zúcar is a town crossed by the Arroyo del Carrizal and known mainly for the old construction that was in the town, where currently only the remains of what used to be an Alcazaba remain. From this point there is a view of the old Alcazaba.

The Jabalcón Canal is an artificial construction that takes water from the Negratín reservoir and takes it to nearby towns. At this point, the arid lands of the mountains contrast with the spaces of vegetation thanks to the water from the dam.

The municipality of Cortes de Baza is located in the north of the province of Granada. It comes from the Arabic "Qultis" whose meaning was used in the past to refer to a small agricultural and livestock holding, like a country house.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route, approximately 101 kilometers long with a significant elevation gain of 1,230 meters. It is considered a hard ride and requires a very good fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by mostly well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride for road bikes. However, be aware that some segments in the broader area around Embalse del Negratín might be unpaved and difficult for road bikes.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience incredibly diverse and striking scenery. Key highlights include the beautiful View of Negratín Reservoir, with its aquamarine to cobalt blue waters, and the arid, desert-like landscapes of the Hoya de Baza, often referred to as 'badlands' or the Gorafe Desert. You'll also see impressive canyons and may catch distant views of the snow-capped Sierra Nevada mountains. Other notable points include the settlement of Zújar, the Canal de Jabalcón, and Cortes de Baza.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Pozo Alcón. You can typically find parking within the town of Pozo Alcón to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the arid, desert-like landscapes of the Hoya de Baza, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are generally the best times. Temperatures are more moderate, making the long distance and elevation gain more enjoyable. Summers can be extremely hot, and winters can be chilly.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: This route traverses the Geoparque de Granada. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Geoparque. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas and private property.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires a 'very good fitness' level due to its length (over 100 km) and significant elevation gain (1,230 m). It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails, including parts of the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), El Camino Espiritual del Sur, and the Transandalus.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on the roads themselves, it's generally not advisable to bring dogs on such a long and demanding road cycling trip due to safety concerns with traffic and the physical demands on the animal. If you do, ensure they are safely secured and hydrated.

faqs.question: Are there any unique geological features to see along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route is rich in unique geological formations. You'll encounter impressive canyons and traverse areas of 'badlands' within the Hoya de Baza and Gorafe Desert, showcasing dramatic arid landscapes and distinctive rock formations.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through settlements like Zújar and Cortes de Baza. These towns offer opportunities to refill water bottles, find cafes, or grab a meal. However, given the route's length, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially between towns.
